listings.dealer: Cherry Hill Dodge Chrysler Jeep RAM

top10.introHtml: Refreshed 2026-07-07. Scanned 85 raw listings across Cars.com (27), CarGurus (21), and AutoTrader (37). After filtering 2-seat roadsters (Miata, SL-Class, Boxster, Z4, etc.), 46 4-seat convertibles remain. 100% image coverage across all sources. 4 Mercedes SL-Class (2-seat roadsters) filtered from Cars.com (27 raw). 2-seat roadsters and non-convertible body styles filtered from AutoTrader (37 raw). TrueCar skipped per task config (body-style filter broken). Note: 2010 Ford Mustang (Reading, PA) appeared in Cars.com results but measured 40mi — excluded. 1 qualifying 2005+ Mustang found within radius.

picks.whyGood: The B8 Audi A5 Cabriolet is the budget MVP of this search — 56k miles on a 4-seat drop-top with a strong 2.0T and quattro AWD for just $9,990 is exceptional value. A5 Cabriolets are rare and hold their shape well. Good Deal badge validates the price.
picks.whatToVerify: Full convertible top cycle (open AND close, watch for hesitation or rattles). Check headliner and rear carpet for water stains — a telltale of top-seal failure. Inspect rear window seam. Pull timing chain service history (B8 2.0T is chain-driven but tensioner should be inspected at this mileage). MMI screen and electronics check.
picks.bottomLine: Best-value 4-seat convertible in the search — low mileage, AWD, and a modern platform for under $10k.

picks.whyGood: The E93 328i is the gold standard of sub-$15k convertibles — balanced rear-wheel-drive chassis, 230hp inline-6, proper 4 seats, and a hydraulic soft top that's mature and reliable by 2010. Only 9 miles from Media at a proper dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: N52 engine oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing). Soft-top hydraulic rams (look for slow close or fluid on the pump). Rear window de-fog function. Check for cooling system work (thermostat, water pump). Water staining on rear seat leather from top seam.
picks.bottomLine: The right car for the right money — close, reliable platform, and a genuinely fun drive.

picks.whyGood: Newest convertible in the top 10 by a wide margin — 2015 F57 platform with only 55k miles, clean title and no accidents confirmed, and just 8 miles away in Easttown. Fun around-town character with surprising top-down presence.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle test (MINI cloth tops can develop window separation). Check rear seat headroom and access. Verify no recalled items outstanding. MINI reliability varies — check service records for timing chain tensioner (prince-era engines had issues, F57 should be cleaner).
picks.bottomLine: Closest, newest, cleanest pick — great for someone who wants a no-drama fun daily drop-top.

picks.whyGood: 51,000 miles on a V8 E64 grand tourer convertible for $11k is almost suspiciously good. The 645Ci is one of BMW's best-looking cars ever made, with real 4-seat space and 325hp N62 V8 thunder. Only 5 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: N62 engine VANOS unit inspection and valve stem seals (known oil consumer). Check for SMG or standard auto — prefer the 6-speed auto (GA6HP26). Soft-top hydraulics full cycle test. IBS (intelligent battery sensor) and electrical gremlins — bring a ISTA-capable scanner. Verify why mileage is so low at 22 years old.
picks.bottomLine: V8 grand tourer at a steal — stunning looks and real performance, but demands careful pre-purchase inspection.

picks.whyGood: The S197 Mustang V6 is the most bulletproof fun-car engine in this search — the 305hp Cyclone V6 launched Ford's reliability renaissance and routinely hits 200k+ miles. Convertible body with proper rear seat. Well under $11k in West Chester.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle — S197 tops are mechanical and durable but check the latch mechanism and weather seal. Rear seat water staining from top seal. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff (common, often needs fluid). Rocker rust inspection underneath.
picks.bottomLine: The no-nonsense fun-car pick — proven powertrain, easy to maintain, American V6 sound that won't quit.

picks.whyGood: The C70 retractable hardtop is a head-turner with no soft-top worries — the folding roof mechanism is elegant and robust by 2011. Swedish-built T5 five-cylinder turbo has real character. At 93k miles and $9,990 with a clean, quiet personality.
picks.whatToVerify: Full roof cycle (open, close, emergency release test). Check all hydraulic seals on the roof mechanism — leaks are costly. Inspect roof drains (front and rear) for blockage. T5 timing belt service history (critical — should be done by ~100k). Check for P0016/P0017 cam correlation codes.
picks.bottomLine: The sophisticated choice — a retractable hardtop grand tourer with Scandinavian style at a solid price.

picks.whyGood: The Cascada is the most underrated 4-seat convertible in America — Opel-engineered, genuinely well-built, and at 71k miles on a 2016 it's comparatively fresh. Premium trim means heated leather, navigation, and a Bose stereo. Lots of car for $11,195.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top operation — Cascada electric roofs are quiet but check for hesitation. Roof drain tubes (front corners) — confirm no standing water in trunk. Verify no airbag/TPMS warning lights. Check for any GM technical service bulletins on the 1.6T engine (turbo actuator).
picks.bottomLine: The overlooked gem — refined, spacious, and properly modern for a 2016 convertible at this price.

picks.whyGood: The A207 E350 Cabriolet is arguably the most beautiful car in this entire search — proper 4-seat grand touring, 3.5L V6, and a folding soft top with acoustic roof lining. Clean title and no accidents verified through Cars.com. At $11,995 it's as close to aspirational as this budget gets.
picks.whatToVerify: Top mechanism full cycle (look for roof-module codes). Check all four roof drains — blocked drains cause water in the trunk and footwells. Interior for water staining. Mercedes maintenance costs at this mileage (120k+ service). Check for ABC suspension warning lights if equipped.
picks.bottomLine: The grand touring pick — genuinely stunning, genuinely roomy, genuinely expensive to maintain if anything goes wrong.

picks.whyGood: Great Deal badge, lowest-priced BMW in the search, and 2013 means the more refined F-series adjacent platform. At $8,326 there's room in the budget for a solid pre-buy inspection and any immediate maintenance catches. New PA inspection through March 2027 noted in the listing.
picks.whatToVerify: With 145k miles, budget $300–500 for a full PPI. Check: cooling system (thermostat, water pump), valve cover gasket, soft-top hydraulic rams, and any active fault codes. Look for rear seat water intrusion. Rocker rust check.
picks.bottomLine: The value bet — Great Deal price on a proper BMW convertible, but high miles demand a thorough inspection.

picks.whyGood: The B7 A4 Cabriolet with quattro AWD is a genuinely practical 4-season fun car. At 62k miles it's relatively low for a 2007. The 2.0T engine is well-proven by this point. Sold by an established Willow Grove dealer.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing belt (critical — B7 2.0T is belt-driven; confirm service at ~80k interval). Soft-top condition and full operation cycle. CVT vs 6-speed manual — prefer manual. Audi electricals: check MMI, HVAC blend doors, window regulators. Quattro rear diff service.
picks.bottomLine: AWD 4-seat convertible at a reasonable price — practical for shoulder-season top-down driving, but timing belt is non-negotiable.

manual10.introHtml: Expanded to 100-mile radius from ZIP 19063 to find manual-transmission convertibles. Scanned 48 Cars.com results + 22 AutoTrader results (both filtered to manual transmission). After removing 2-seat roadsters (Miata, Z3, Boxster, S2000, Solstice, Corvette C4, SLK, MGB, MR2, 350Z, Crossfire, Saturn Sky), 22 qualifying 4-seat manual convertibles remain. Top 10 ranked below. Budget $7,000–$15,000.

picks.whyGood: 37,205 miles on an LS1-powered Z28 is almost unheard of — the 5.7L V8 and Tremec T56 6-speed were refined by 1999 and hadn't even broken in yet at this odometer. The 4th-gen Camaro is a proper 4-seat convertible with real muscle-car presence. Under $9,500 for this mileage is the best dollars-per-LS1 ratio in the search.
picks.whatToVerify: With 37K miles at 27 years old, renew coolant, brake fluid, and differential fluid regardless. Check T56 for notchy 1-2 shift (common, usually minor). Inspect soft top for UV degradation and seal condition. Verify rear leaf spring mounts and torque arm bushings. Check for LS1 oil leaks at rear main and valve covers. Low miles on a classic means long storage periods — check for dry rot on rubber hoses and seals.
picks.bottomLine: The mileage king — an LS1 V8 with 37K miles in a convertible is the rarest find in this entire search.

picks.whyGood: 44,000 miles on a 1994 SN95 Mustang GT is time-capsule territory. The 5.0L V8 with 5-speed manual is one of Ford's most bulletproof combinations, and the SN95 finally gave the Mustang proper structural rigidity after the Fox years. Clean title and no accidents confirmed. Only 29 miles from Media.
picks.whatToVerify: At 44K miles after 32 years, check clutch hydraulics (master and slave cylinders dry out from disuse more than mileage). Inspect soft top rear window for crazing or yellowing. Brake rotors and rubber hoses need inspection regardless of mileage. Listen on cold start for lifter tick. Check convertible top bows for corrosion.
picks.bottomLine: Time-capsule SN95 — low miles, clean history, close to home. If it passes PPI this is a buy.

picks.whyGood: Only 15,000 miles on a 2012 — that's just over 1,000 miles per year. The FIAT 500C cabriolet has a folding fabric roof that opens the full cabin, 4 seats, and a 5-speed manual with the 1.4L MultiAir engine. At $8,900 with essentially no wear, this is the lowest-mileage car in the entire manual search by a wide margin.
picks.whatToVerify: Very low annual mileage means extended storage periods — check brake calipers and rotors for seizure, rubber hoses for dry rot, clutch hydraulics for seal degradation, and timing belt condition (replace regardless of mileage at this age). Inspect the cabriolet roof fold mechanism carefully. Check for rodent damage in wiring harness.
picks.bottomLine: Lowest-mileage car in the search — inspect carefully for storage-related issues but if it's clean, it's a steal.

picks.whyGood: The John Cooper Works convertible is the hot version of the R57 — 208hp turbocharged 1.6L, 6-speed manual, and the JCW sport suspension. At 46K miles it still has plenty of life. 4 proper seats, sharp handling, and the highest fun-per-dollar ratio in the compact segment. Cars.com confirmed manual transmission.
picks.whatToVerify: JCW engines run harder than base MINI units — check for oil consumption and timing chain tensioner condition (replace proactively if not documented). Full soft top cycle and rear window seal. MINI electronics: scan for active codes. Thermostat housing crack is common on R56-era engines. Turbocharger health check.
picks.bottomLine: Hot 208hp convertible for under $10k — the JCW is the sharpest-driving car in this search.

picks.whyGood: The 335is is the hot-rod E93 — BMW tuned the N54 twin-turbo to 320hp and gave it unique sport tuning over the standard 335i. At 90,803 miles and $14,995 it's at the top of the budget but delivers real performance: 0-60 in under 5 seconds in a proper 4-seat drop-top. Confirm the 6-speed manual (not DCT) before the test drive.
picks.whatToVerify: N54 known issues: high-pressure fuel pump (HPFP), charge pipe blow-off under boost, and wastegate rattle. Confirm 6-speed manual (some 335is are DCT). Check E93 soft top hydraulic rams and roof module. Oil leaks at valve cover and oil filter housing. Budget for walnut-blast on intake valves (GDI carbon buildup).
picks.bottomLine: The performance pick — 320hp twin-turbo BMW convertible at budget limit, demands a thorough inspection but delivers real speed.

picks.whyGood: The E46 330Ci is one of the last truly analog BMWs — naturally aspirated M54 2.5L inline-6 with 225hp, hydraulic steering, 50/50 weight balance, and proper 4-seat hydraulic soft top. Under $10k for a manual E46 convertible is strong value for a driver who wants to feel connected to the road.
picks.whatToVerify: M54 oil leaks are near-certain at this mileage: valve cover, DISA valve O-ring, oil filter housing gasket. Full cooling system overhaul (thermostat, water pump, expansion tank — plastic parts fail). Soft top hydraulic rams and pump. Check rear subframe mounts for cracks (E46 known issue). Verify accident history — these were popular with young owners.
picks.bottomLine: The analog driver's car — pure E46 feel under $10k, but budget for deferred maintenance and potential subframe inspection.

picks.whyGood: The R57 Cooper S convertible hits the sweet spot: turbocharged 1.6L with 172hp, 6-speed manual, 4 proper seats, no accidents per AutoTrader, and only 65K miles. Josie's in Gilbertsville is a known local dealer. At $8,452 it's among the lower-priced cars in the manual search and is close enough for an easy test drive.
picks.whatToVerify: R56-era Cooper S: timing chain tensioner is critical — replace proactively if not documented. Thermostat housing for cracks, turbo oil feed line for weeping. Full soft top cycle and rear window seal integrity. Clutch bite point check. Coolant reservoir for cracks.
picks.bottomLine: Close-to-home option with clean history — solid price, real Cooper S performance, and a local dealer to negotiate with.

picks.whyGood: The Saab 9-3 Aero Convertible is one of the most overlooked 4-seat manual drop-tops available. The Aero trim gets 210hp from the 2.0T turbo and a 6-speed manual — real performance that most buyers skip because they're fixated on German badges. Only 14 miles from Media, sold by a legitimate Audi franchised dealer. 81K miles is respectable.
picks.whatToVerify: Saab 2.0T: check for oil sludge caused by neglected oil changes — pull the cap and look. Turbo hoses for cracks. Full soft top operation and seal condition. Scan for DTC codes. Saab parts availability has improved through GM-era commonality but confirm no open recalls.
picks.bottomLine: The underdog pick — closest manual on the list, sold by a real franchised dealer, and genuinely fun if you want something different.

picks.whyGood: The 1997 Z28 uses the LT1 5.7L V8 — the last generation before the LS1 arrived in 1998, with 285hp and the T56 6-speed manual. 73K miles at $11,895 is solid value. These 4th-gen Z28 convertibles are gaining collector attention as '90s nostalgia cars, and the soft top is the easy-to-service manual variety.
picks.whatToVerify: LT1 opti-spark ignition distributor is the #1 known failure point — confirm it's been replaced or budget $400 for the job. Check for coolant entering the opti-spark housing. Rear leaf spring bushings and torque arm wear. Soft top condition and weatherstripping seal. Check brake proportioning valve.
picks.bottomLine: Pre-LS muscle at 73K miles — opti-spark is the known gremlin, address it first and the car is bulletproof.

picks.whyGood: The most modern car in the entire manual search by a decade. The 2019 S550 EcoBoost with the 2.3L turbo-4 and 6-speed manual makes 310hp and brings everything the older cars can't: Apple CarPlay, SYNC 3, heated leather seats, active exhaust, and modern crash safety ratings. At $14,995 it's at the budget limit but represents a totally different reliability and comfort tier.
picks.whatToVerify: S550 EcoBoost: confirm 6-speed manual (not 10-speed auto). Check MT-82 transmission for notchy 1-2 shift (common, aftermarket shifter solves it). EcoBoost intercooler and charge pipe for leaks at 123K. Rear convertible top latch mechanism. Verify all SYNC, driver-assist, and active exhaust systems work. Soft top full cycle.
picks.bottomLine: The modern pick — only car in this search with Apple CarPlay and current safety features, and the 6-speed manual keeps it engaging.

mustangs.introHtml: Only 1 qualifying Ford Mustang convertible found within 35 miles in budget that meets the 2005+ (S197/S550) requirement. The 1987 and 1994 Mustangs in the overall listings are Fox-body and SN-95 generations — classic fun but excluded from this section per criteria.

picks.whyGood: The 2012 S197 V6 is one of the best-value Mustang convertibles ever made — 305hp Cyclone V6 easily beats the old 4.6 V8 in economy and longevity, under $11k with ~97k miles. Sold 12 miles away. The S197 body style (2010–2014) is arguably the cleanest generation.
picks.whatToVerify: S197-specific: full top latch cycle and weather seal condition. Rear seat water staining from top seal failure. Check 8.8 rear axle for clunk on takeoff — fluid change often cures it. Inspect spring perches for rust (known S197 issue). Check for any cam phaser noise (V6 doesn't have this issue like the 3-valve V8, but verify).
picks.bottomLine: The only 2005+ Mustang convertible in budget within 35 miles — and it's a good one.
